pattern: Prim's algorithm builds a minimum spanning tree over a complete graph where every edge weight is the Manhattan distance between the two endpoints.
hints: Connect all points with minimum total edge cost — this is exactly a minimum spanning tree problem on a graph where every pair of points is an edge.
With up to 1000 points the full edge list has nearly a million edges; Prim's algorithm with a min-heap or a simple linear scan of candidates is efficient enough.
In the linear-scan variant, maintain the cheapest known edge to each unvisited node and update it each time a new node joins the tree.

edge_inputs.description: five points from the classic example
points with negative coordinates spanning all four quadrants
points clustered near origin so many short edges compete
two points on the same row

stress.description: random points scattered across the coordinate range
